utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: includes provider, monthly and annual costs, key features. The format requires 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'—costs are present; features suggest both physical (evacuation, telemedicine) and mental (psychological support). This appears fully specified per format. 2) property_coverage: includes monthly cost and key features, but no annual cost. The format requires 'home/contents insurance with costs'—at least some costs are present; typically both monthly/annual are acceptable, and it explicitly includes home/contents. Arguably sufficiently specified with costs. 3) travel_coverage: includes provider, monthly cost, key features; no annual cost. The format requires 'travel insurance with costs'—cost provided monthly, so acceptable. 4) additional_services: includes an enhancement service with provider, monthly cost, key features; costs present. 5) total_budget: includes monthly_total and annual_total with notes—complete monthly/annual cost breakdown present. All 5 items appear and include costs; none are empty. The only potential gap is missing annual costs for property and travel, but the format does not strictly mandate both monthly and annual per item, only that costs be included. Therefore, all 5 items are successfully included and sufficiently specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three main policy providers and one additional service. I matched each to the corresponding item in the ground-truth ratings by name. For health_coverage and additional_services.mental_health_support, the provider is 'EliteCare Premium', which corresponds to 'EliteCare Premium Plan' in both primary_health_coverage.physical_health and primary_health_coverage.mental_health, each with rating 10. For property_coverage, the plan cites 'ParisHome Luxury Coverage', which does not exist in the ground-truth list; therefore, no rating can be mapped. For travel_coverage, the provider 'FamilyGlobal Platinum' is also not present in the ground-truth travel_insurance options; thus, no rating can be mapped. Only items with exact name matches are included in RATINGS.
